semanticCore: Idle talk, vanity, mistake, cancellation, or error
quranicRelevance: Vain, idle talk in Dunya or Hell (Lagw - 'wa itha sami'oo al-laghwa a'radhoo 'anhu'), and shouting to interrupt the Quran ('wal-ghaw feehi').

keyDerivations.meaning: Idle talk, vanity, nonsense, void speech, or mistake
To talk idly, speak nonsense, make noise, or interrupt

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَالْغَوْا فِيهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَغْلِبُونَ (41:26): And shout/make noise during it (the Quran) so you may overcome (pagan obstruction strategy).
discardedNoise: Juristic status of oaths uttered in error (لغو اليمين) in legal courts.
summaryEn: The root represents useless, empty speech (Lagw), depicting the noise used by pagans to block out scriptural warnings and the absence of such vanity in Paradise.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر اللغو والساقط من القول، مصوراً الصخب الجاهلي المصطنع للتشويش على القرآن (والغوا فيه)، وتنزيه الجنة عنه.
rawText: اللُّغَةُ The genuine language of the Arabs; which is the classical language. لَاغٍ : see مَائِرٌ. لَاغَاهُ 3 He jested, or joked, with him; inf. n. مُلَاغَاةٌ. (A, TA.) You say, لَاغَاهُ فِى البَيْعِ. (A, TA, art. كيس.) لَغْوٌ , applied to speech, &c., Nought; of no account; (M, K;) unprofitable. (M.) === اليَمِينُ اللَّغْوُ: see KT, voce يَمِينٌ. لُغَوِىٌّ Of, or relating to, the genuine language of the Arabs. مَعْنًى لُغَوِىٌّ A genuine lexicological meaning. لُغَيَّةٌ A word of weak authority. (TA, voce جِرَاب, et passim.)
